_comment: Benchmark scenario registry for LLM agent evaluation. Traces reference files in demo/. problem_type drives prompt context and result grouping in benchmark/metrics.py.

scenarios.name: mem-slight
scenarios.description: 3 pods each requesting 33Gi memory on 32Gi/node KWOK nodes — all pods stay Pending. Agent must reduce memory requests to fit under the per-node limit.

scenarios.name: mem-heavy
scenarios.description: 3 pods each requesting 40Gi memory on 32Gi/node KWOK nodes — all pods stay Pending. Agent must reduce memory multiple times (40Gi → 32Gi requires more than one step).

scenarios.name: cpu-slight
scenarios.description: 3 pods each requesting 17000m CPU (51 cores total) on 16-CPU KWOK nodes — pods are unschedulable. Agent must reduce CPU requests below 16000m per pod.

scenarios.name: cpu-heavy
scenarios.description: 3 pods each requesting 20000m CPU (60 cores total) on 16-CPU KWOK nodes — far over capacity. Agent must reduce CPU multiple times to get all pods scheduled.

scenarios.name: cpu-mem-both
scenarios.description: 3 pods each over-requesting both CPU (17000m > 16 CPU/node) and memory (33Gi > 32Gi/node). Agent must reduce both dimensions independently to get pods scheduled.

scenarios.name: replicas-over
scenarios.description: 5 replicas running when the target is 3 — pods schedule fine but replica count is wrong. Agent must issue a scale_down_replicas action to reach the target.

scenarios.name: scale-up
scenarios.description: 1 underprovisioned pod running when the target is 3. Agent must scale up replicas to reach the target of 3 ready pods.

scenarios.name: scale-up-v2
scenarios.description: 2 underprovisioned pods running when the target is 3. Agent must add one more replica to reach the target of 3 ready pods.

scenarios.name: baseline-normalized
scenarios.description: Healthy baseline: 2 replicas with well-within-limits resources (500m CPU, 512Mi memory). Used for local development and sanity-checking agent no-op behaviour.

scenarios.name: baseline-dev
scenarios.description: Synthetic development trace: 2 replicas, 500m CPU, 512Mi memory, all in the 'default' namespace. Used for integration testing — observe() returns {ready: 2, pending: 0, total: 2} once pods start.
